content.problemStatement:
You have a cricket team with NNN players, each numbered from 111 to NNN.

The batting lineup is a specific order of these players in which they bat, where, the first **two** are the openers.

When a wicket falls, the next player in the lineup comes in to bat. If there is no person left in the lineup when a wicket falls, the team is considered all out.

Note that:


The team is considered all-out after the fall of N−1N-1N−1 wickets.
At any time before all-out, **two** batsman are present on the field, either of which can be out first.
Each player has a known scoring ability, represented by max⁡(i,j)\max(i, j)max(i,j), where iii is the player's number and jjj is their position in the lineup.

The **total score** of the lineup is the sum of all individual scores.

You're given an array PPP of size N−1N-1N−1, indicating the sequence of **wickets falling**.

Your task is to find the **maximum total score** possible for a lineup that is capable of producing this given sequence of wickets falling.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the number of players in the team.
The next line contains N−1N-1N−1 space separated integers, representing the fall of wickets sequence.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer, the **maximum total score** possible for a lineup that produces this given sequence of falling wickets.
constraints: It is guaranteed that PPP contains unique integers from 111 to NNN.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.
